fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Creation Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that the date compression took place.

fields.name: Table Caption
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the table that was compressed.

fields.name: Period Length
fields.tooltip: Specifies the time interval of entries combined into one for the period defined in the Starting Date and Ending Date fields in the batch job.

fields.name: Ending Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last date in the period for which entries were compressed.

fields.name: No. of New Records
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of new entries that were created by the date compression.

fields.name: No. Records Deleted
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of entries that were deleted during the date compression.

fields.name: User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who posted the entry, to be used, for example, in the change log.

fields.name: Filter
fields.tooltip: Specifies the filters that were placed on the date compression.

fields.name: Starting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the first date in the period for which entries were compressed.

fields.name: Table ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the table that was compressed.

fields.name: Register No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the register that was created by the date compression and that contains the compressed entries.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the source code that specifies where the entry was created.

fields.name: Retain Field Contents
fields.tooltip: Specifies a list of the fields whose contents the user chose to retain in the date compression.

fields.name: Retain Totals
fields.tooltip: Specifies a list of the quantity fields that the user chose to retain when they ran the date compression batch job.
